entries.name: Storm Shield Core
entries.vanilla_overlap: none: vanilla 1.21.9 has no force-field or multiblock shield; the handheld shield item is the nearest, unrelated analogue
entries.summary: Multiblock Storm Shield generator: a copper core structure that projects a charged protective dome around a base, consuming stored charge while active.

entries.name: Shield Projectile Interception
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: the vanilla shield item blocks projectiles for a single player; the Storm Shield intercepts arrows/tridents/fireballs across the whole dome
entries.summary: Projectile interception for the Storm Shield: incoming projectiles are stopped at the dome surface with a visible ripple effect at the impact point.

entries.name: Shield Mob Repulsion
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla mob-repulsion field exists; nearest analogues are warden sonic knockback and wind charge bursts
entries.summary: Hostile mob repulsion for the Storm Shield: hostiles touching the dome are pushed back and take shock damage that scales with stored charge.

entries.name: Lightning-Capture Rods
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: the vanilla lightning_rod (since 1.17, still present in 1.21.9) redirects strikes but discards the energy; capture rods convert strikes into stored charge
entries.summary: Lightning-capture rod block that attracts lightning strikes and converts them into charge fed to connected storage.

entries.name: Leyden Jar Batteries
entries.vanilla_overlap: none: vanilla 1.21.9 has no energy-storage block; copper_bulb is only a light with redstone stages
entries.summary: Leyden Jar battery blocks that store captured charge in tiered capacities, with visible fill levels and comparator output.

entries.name: Oxidation Weapons
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: vanilla 1.21.9 added copper_sword/copper_axe (full copper tool set); Cuprum oxidation weapons add oxidation stages that change damage and effects, and can be waxed to lock a stage
entries.summary: Oxidation-mechanic copper weapons that extend and differentiate from the vanilla 1.21.9 copper gear: stats and on-hit effects shift as the weapon oxidizes.

entries.name: Oxidation Armor
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: vanilla 1.21.9 added copper_helmet/chestplate/leggings/boots and copper horse armor; Cuprum oxidation armor adds visual+mechanical oxidation stages, set bonuses and waxing
entries.summary: Oxidation-mechanic copper armor set that extends and differentiates from the vanilla 1.21.9 copper armor: protection and set bonuses evolve with oxidation stage.

entries.name: Charged Exoskeleton
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla powered exoskeleton; wind-charge jump boosts and elytra are the nearest movement analogues
entries.summary: Charge-powered movement exoskeleton granting boosted jumps and a directional dash while charged.

entries.name: Tesla Turrets
entries.vanilla_overlap: none: vanilla 1.21.9 has no automated turret block
entries.summary: Charge-powered Tesla turret blocks that arc shock damage to nearby hostile mobs while drawing from connected batteries.

entries.name: Hovering Mag-Rails
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: vanilla powered_rail accelerates grounded carts; mag-rails levitate carts and target roughly 3x powered-rail speed
entries.summary: Magnetic hover rails: charged rail blocks that levitate minecarts and propel them at about three times powered-rail speed.

entries.name: Pneumatic Item Tubes
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: hoppers/droppers/water streams move items slowly or invisibly; pneumatic tubes are fast point-to-point transport with visible windows showing items in transit
entries.summary: Pneumatic item transport tubes with visible glass windows so items can be watched travelling through the network.

entries.name: Programmable Copper Golems
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: the vanilla 1.21.9 copper golem only moves items from copper chests into other chests; Cuprum golems are programmable for broader utility tasks (harvesting, hauling, button duty)
entries.summary: Programmable utility copper golems extending the vanilla 1.21.9 copper golem with configurable task programs.

entries.name: Copper Fans
entries.vanilla_overlap: none: vanilla 1.21.9 has no fan/air-stream block; breeze wind charges are the nearest air-push analogue
entries.summary: Copper fan blocks producing directional air streams that push entities and items, toggleable by redstone.

entries.name: Copper Grappling Hook
entries.vanilla_overlap: none: no vanilla grappling hook; reeling entities with a fishing rod is the nearest analogue
entries.summary: Copper grappling hook item that latches onto blocks and pulls the player toward the anchor point.

entries.name: Ore Detector
entries.vanilla_overlap: none: vanilla has no ore-finding tool or block
entries.summary: Handheld charged ore detector that pings nearby ore veins with directional feedback.

entries.name: Weighted Plates
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: vanilla light/heavy weighted pressure plates (gold/iron) have fixed response curves; Cuprum plates have player-configurable mass and entity-count thresholds
entries.summary: Configurable weighted pressure plates that trigger on adjustable mass or entity-count thresholds.

entries.name: Backpack Personal Shield
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: totem of undying is a one-shot death save; the backpack shield is a rechargeable wearable damage barrier
entries.summary: Wearable backpack that projects a miniaturized personal Storm Shield around the player, draining its internal charge as it absorbs damage.

entries.name: XP Collector Drones
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: vanilla has no XP automation; the sculk catalyst converts mob XP into sculk but cannot collect orbs for the player
entries.summary: Small charged drones that gather dropped experience orbs and ferry them back to their home station or owner.

entries.name: Conductive Climbing Wire
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: redstone dust only carries a 15-block signal on floors; conductive wire carries charge over long distances and climbs walls and ceilings
entries.summary: Wall-climbing conductive copper wire for long-distance charge transmission between rods, jars and machines.

entries.name: Oxidation Copper Spikes
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: vanilla has no spike trap block; pointed dripstone, berry bushes and magma deal passive contact damage without scaling
entries.summary: Copper spike blocks whose contact damage scales with their oxidation stage; waxing freezes the stage.

entries.name: Weather Manipulator
entries.vanilla_overlap: none: weather changes only through the /weather command or the natural cycle; vanilla survival has no weather-control block
entries.summary: Charge-hungry weather manipulator block that can summon or dispel thunderstorms, closing the loop with lightning capture.

entries.name: Dynamic Handbook
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: the vanilla recipe book only lists unlocked recipes; the handbook is a full dynamic guide generated from the content catalog
entries.summary: Complete dynamic in-game handbook documenting every Cuprum system, generated from the catalog so it can never go stale.

entries.name: Copper Bus Bar
entries.summary: Flat surface-mounted conduit linking adjacent machines; its glow state tracks live load so players can read flow at a glance.

entries.name: Insulated Cable
entries.summary: Rubber-clad wire that never shocks entities and works underwater, at reduced throughput.

entries.name: Overhead Pylon
entries.summary: Pylon pairs span up to 32 blocks with an aerial line, carrying charge across gaps without block-by-block wiring.

entries.name: Charge Meter
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: comparator reads containers, not energy
entries.summary: Analog needle dial showing network load and emitting a proportional comparator signal.

entries.name: Potential Gauge
entries.summary: Handheld multimeter; use on any charge node prints stored charge, in/out rates and network id.

entries.name: Small Leyden Cell
entries.summary: Early cheap battery that can be carried while charged and placed in banks beside jars.

entries.name: Grand Leyden Array
entries.summary: 3×3×3 multiblock battery bank whose capacity scales with the jars inserted into its 27 rack slots.

entries.name: Crank Dynamo
entries.summary: Hand-cranked generator: hold use to produce a charge trickle before any automation exists (active use, so it outputs 4× passive baseline).

entries.name: Thermo Junction
entries.summary: Generates charge from a heat differential: fire or lava below, water or ice above; output scales with the gradient (lava+water is the 5 Cg/t passive baseline).

entries.name: Windlass Turbine
entries.summary: Tall rotor generating from the mod's global wind vector; output scales with altitude and weather. Introduces the per-day wind vector consumed by WEA-07/08 and DEC-09.

entries.name: Water Wheel
entries.summary: Wheel mounted over flowing water generating the steady passive baseline from flow width.

entries.name: Solar Verdigris Panel
entries.summary: Solar panel whose output rises with its oxidation stage, rewarding players who let it weather unwaxed.

entries.name: Surge Protector
entries.summary: Absorbs lightning backfeed spikes that would damage machines, sacrificing a replaceable fuse item instead.

entries.name: Step-Up Transformer
entries.summary: Converts a line to high voltage for long hauls; a paired step-down is required before machines accept the feed.

entries.name: Charge Coupler
entries.summary: Beams charge wirelessly to a paired coupler up to 8 blocks away through air, shown as a visible arc.

entries.name: Redstone–Charge Converter
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: redstone signals exist, energy does not
entries.summary: Bidirectional bridge: buffered charge emits a redstone signal and a redstone input gates charge flow.

entries.name: Breaker Switch
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: lever toggles redstone, not energy
entries.summary: Manual segment isolator that auto-trips on overload and must be hand-reset, protecting sub-grids.

entries.name: Load Balancer
entries.summary: GUI node assigning machines to priority tiers (defense > logistics > misc) so brownouts starve low tiers first.

entries.name: Battery Pack
entries.summary: Portable charge store that trickle-charges chargeable gadgets in the carrier's inventory and refills at jars.

entries.name: Induction Plinth
entries.summary: Charges items hovering above it and any exoskeleton wearer standing on it, hands-free (fast charger drawing from network storage).

entries.name: Grounding Rod
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: lightning_rod redirects strikes, not stored charge
entries.summary: Safely vents excess network charge into the ground, preventing overload damage.

entries.name: Static Sentinel Lamp
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: copper_bulb is a redstone-toggleable light whose brightness tracks oxidation; the Sentinel instead encodes live network health and alarms
entries.summary: Corridor lamp that encodes network supply status in flicker patterns and sounds a blackout alarm — status infrastructure, not general lighting.

entries.name: Charge Teleprinter
entries.summary: Prints timestamped network events (overload, jar full, breaker trip) onto paper-tape items players can file.

entries.name: Franklin Kite
entries.summary: Throwable storm kite entity that channels one natural strike down its string to a linked capture rod.

entries.name: Verdigris Ingot
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: vanilla copper_ingot exists; verdigris smelts only from stage-3 oxidized copper
entries.summary: New ingot smelted from fully oxidized cut copper, the entry ticket to the patina-mechanics gear tier.

entries.name: Cuprite Alloy
entries.summary: Machine-frame alloy ingot used in W6+ machine recipes; registered as the output of the Alloy Kiln (OXI-03), which is built from vanilla materials.

entries.name: Alloy Kiln
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: furnace smelts single inputs; the kiln fuses two inputs
entries.summary: Dual-input furnace built from bricks and copper (vanilla-material bootstrap) that fuses metal pairs into alloys; an optional charge fast path halves the time for a fixed upfront cost.

entries.name: Patina Extract
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla item ages copper; only time or lightning changes stages
entries.summary: Bottled agent that instantly advances the oxidation stage of a clicked copper block or held Cuprum gear by one; bootstraps from vanilla materials (4 stage-3 oxidized cut copper + 1 glass bottle).

entries.name: Anti-Patina Wax Blend
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: honeycomb waxes one block per use
entries.summary: Improved wax protecting whole stacks and armor pieces; axe-scraping returns wax flakes.

entries.name: Electrolytic Refinery
entries.summary: Charge-powered refinery splitting raw copper into extra ingots plus trace silver nuggets used in fine components (TES-10 weave).

entries.name: Comminution Mill
entries.summary: Grinding machine that doubles copper ore output via dust (copper-only doubling).

entries.name: Patina Transfer Bath
entries.summary: Basin that extracts patina stages from one gear piece into a reservoir and applies them to another — deliberate stage crafting instead of waiting on weather (a distinct process on top of the U06/U07 stage component, not a recount of it).

entries.name: Verdigris Blade
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: vanilla copper_sword has fixed stats
entries.summary: Sword that trades base damage for an armor-shredding corrosive edge as it oxidizes, applying the canonical U06 Corroded effect.

entries.name: Patina Pike
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla reach melee weapon; trident is throwable not long
entries.summary: Reach weapon striking at 3.5 blocks whose oxidized form inflicts the canonical U06 Corroded.

entries.name: Galvanic Arc Bow
entries.summary: Bow firing charge bolts without arrows, draining a carried battery; aged form chains a weak arc to a second target.

entries.name: Verdigris Tool Set
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: vanilla copper tools have fixed stats
entries.summary: Pick/axe/shovel/hoe with an oxidation-scaling bonus-drop passive that grows as the tool ages unwaxed.

entries.name: Waxing Station
entries.summary: Workstation that waxes or de-waxes up to a full stack of items or worn armor in one operation.

entries.name: Weathering Chamber
entries.summary: Humidity-plus-charge machine that accelerates oxidation of contained blocks and gear for players who want aged stats now.

entries.name: Casting Table & Molds
entries.summary: Casting station (block) with four mold items (gear, coil, plate, rod) producing the standard component intermediates that W7+ family recipes consume.

entries.name: Conductive Paste
entries.summary: Slime-and-dust paste that joins wires seamlessly and paints flat circuit traces onto solid block faces; also the FX-14 brewing ingredient.

entries.name: Rusted Iron Set
entries.summary: Throwing patina extract on iron blocks creates a crumbling rusted-iron decorative set that slowly sheds flake particles.

entries.name: Noble Copper
entries.summary: Lightning-struck copper blocks can transmute into rare shimmering noble copper, a top-tier recipe ingredient (GEN-14 craters also seed it).

entries.name: Amplifier Coil I–IV
entries.summary: Pylon insert items setting the dome radius tier.

entries.name: Kinetic Modulator
entries.summary: Mode card: dome intercepts only projectiles at reduced upkeep, letting mobs walk through.

entries.name: Biotic Modulator
entries.summary: Mode card: dome repels only hostile mobs at reduced upkeep, letting projectiles through.

entries.name: Weather Modulator
entries.summary: Mode card: natural lightning that would land inside the dome is deflected into the capture network as stored charge.

entries.name: Window Emitter
entries.summary: Redstone-triggered emitter opening a temporary walk-through gap in the dome for sorties and deliveries.

entries.name: Hue Prism
entries.summary: Pylon attachment making the dome surface a live status display: 4 shape-coded patterns encode the active modulator mode and the tint band steps with stored charge — team identity plus at-a-glance, colorblind-readable shield state.

entries.name: Priority Feed Link
entries.summary: Dedicated feed line registering the shield at defense priority so it stays up through network brownouts.

entries.name: Shield Alarm Bell
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: vanilla bell rings manually or on raid
entries.summary: Bell that rings and chat-alerts owners when the dome takes heavy load or runs low.

entries.name: Micro-Dome Projector
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: totem/shield item are the nearest personal defenses
entries.summary: Pocket single-use projector deploying a small personal dome anywhere — an emergency bubble.

entries.name: Resonance Anchor
entries.summary: Multiblock anchor letting two adjacent domes overlap stably, merging their boundaries into a lens intersection.

entries.name: Heat Bloom
entries.summary: Sustained impacts heat the dome; past threshold it flickers open briefly, giving attackers an earned window.

entries.name: Aegis Reader
entries.summary: Wearable HUD widget showing live shield percentage, upkeep and an intercept log while inside the radius.

entries.name: Storm Bastion Banner
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: banners are decorative in vanilla
entries.summary: Team banner on the pedestal making the dome friend/foe aware for one scoreboard team — a dome-wide policy layer, unlike TES-01's per-turret entity filters.

entries.name: Echo Emitter
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: Glowing exists via spectral arrows
entries.summary: On each projectile intercept, emits a sonar ping outlining the shooter through walls.

entries.name: Targeting Card
entries.summary: Programmable filter card inserted into one turret selecting hostiles-only, players, or name-whitelist target classes (per-turret scope; team-wide dome policy is SHD-13).

entries.name: Chain Capacitor
entries.summary: Turret upgrade making each arc fork to nearby secondary targets at reduced per-target damage.

entries.name: Overcharge Injector
entries.summary: Turret burst mode consuming five times the charge per shot to add the base canonical U03 Shock stun proc (later W12 effect extensions stack on top of this base Shock and never replace or re-register it).

entries.name: Tesla Fence Post
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla electric barrier; cactus/berry damage is passive
entries.summary: Fence posts projecting a visible shock curtain between adjacent posts that damages entities crossing it (applies U03 Shock).

entries.name: Arc Welder
entries.summary: Handheld tool repairing golems and machines with charge; deals heavy bonus melee damage to undead.

entries.name: Shock Mine
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla landmine; TNT plus plate is manual
entries.summary: Buried disc discharging an AoE stun-shock (U03) when stepped on, then re-arming from the network.

entries.name: Capacitor Gauntlet
entries.summary: Gauntlet banking charge from your melee hits, released on demand as one lightning punch.

entries.name: Thunderstick
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: crossbow is the vanilla ranged analogue
entries.summary: Single-shot rifle consuming a charged small cell per shot: a piercing beam with hard shooter recoil.

entries.name: Static Aura Emitter
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: thorns enchant reflects generic damage
entries.summary: Wearable module giving melee attackers a shock recoil, draining your portable charge per proc.

entries.name: Faraday Weave
entries.summary: Armor upgrade granting total immunity to shock and lightning — including your own turrets and fences (recipe uses electrolytic silver from OXI-06).

entries.name: Lightning Lure
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: lightning_rod redirects within its small vanilla range
entries.summary: Deployable beacon drawing natural strikes within 64 blocks to itself, concentrating storm harvests.

entries.name: EMP Bomb
entries.summary: Throwable pulse disabling hostile electric infrastructure — the universal counter to electric defense. By default it affects only machines owned by players hostile to the thrower (different team) and unowned hostile turrets; allied, neutral and claim-protected machines are exempt; use against player-owned gear requires config `emp_pvp_enabled` or `cuprum.emp.pvp`.

entries.name: Galvanic Mortar
entries.summary: Emplacement lobbing arcing bombs in a ballistic arc that leave lingering shock fields on impact.

entries.name: Tesla Choir
entries.summary: Singing-arc block producing musical tones from arc frequency, tunable like a note block.

entries.name: Ion Storm Projector
entries.summary: Endgame turret marking entities and calling precise sky strikes onto them during storms.

entries.name: Conductor's Baton
entries.summary: Tuning tool spawning calibrated arcs against its own conjured training dummy and printing exact DPS readouts (QA/tuning aid rather than survival kit, hence stretch).

entries.name: Accelerator Segment
entries.summary: Powered booster segment that visibly speeds items up with a whoosh, stacking to the network speed cap.

entries.name: Filter Valve
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: hopper filtering needs redstone contraptions
entries.summary: Inline valve holding a filter card that passes matching items and bounces the rest back.

entries.name: Sorter Cross
entries.summary: Four-way smart junction routing items by per-side filter priority — the backbone of sorting halls.

entries.name: Overflow Relief
entries.summary: Safety junction diverting items to an attached buffer chest when the destination is full, instead of jamming the line.

entries.name: Vacuum Intake
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: hopper collects only items directly above
entries.summary: Powered intake sucking dropped items from a cone in front of it straight into the tube network.

entries.name: Pneumatic Ejector
entries.summary: Terminal firing arriving items as thrown projectiles with three power settings — an item cannon for gaps and drops.

entries.name: Junction Meter
entries.summary: Inline gauge displaying live items-per-minute throughput and emitting a proportional comparator signal.

entries.name: Fluid Capsules
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: buckets move fluids by hand only
entries.summary: Sealed capsule items carrying fluids through ordinary item tubes, with filler and emptier blocks at the endpoints.

entries.name: Transit Tube
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla player pipe travel
entries.summary: Player-sized glass tubes that whoosh riders along routes — walkable entry, wind-rush travel, safe standing exit.

entries.name: Painting Kit
entries.summary: Dye-based kit color-coding tube runs; filters can route by line color, making spaghetti readable and functional.

entries.name: Pressure Compressor
entries.summary: Raises a network's carrying capacity, hissing audibly when over-pressured and leaking items at the compressor if abused without relief.

entries.name: Relief Vent Grate
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: the vanilla 1.21 copper grate is decorative/waterloggable with no network function
entries.summary: Pressure-relief safety vent: bleeds network over-pressure back to safe levels so compressed networks never leak items, doubling as a live traffic indicator.

entries.name: Capsule Post Office
entries.summary: Address-card mail system: post a capsule to any named receiver anywhere on the connected network.

entries.name: Ender Tube Nexus
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: ender chest shares inventory, not routes
entries.summary: Paired nexus blocks bridging tube networks across dimensions at a steep per-transfer charge cost, with transactional safety and per-nexus rate limiting.

entries.name: Mag-Cart
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: minecart exists; mag-cart hovers with inertia damping and no rail friction
entries.summary: Hovering cart gliding frictionlessly on mag-rails with smooth damped acceleration and no derail jitter.

entries.name: Booster Coil
entries.summary: Powered rail segment accelerating passing carts with a visible field pulse.

entries.name: Brake Damper
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: unpowered golden rail brakes crudely
entries.summary: Deceleration segment bringing carts to station-stop precision without throwing passengers.

entries.name: Station Controller
entries.summary: Station brain scheduling cart departures with a live platform display and departure bell; owns the named-station registry.

entries.name: Mag-Switch Junction
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: vanilla rail T-junctions switch via redstone
entries.summary: Junction rail switching by redstone with a destination-query hook that later route systems (RAIL-10) drive.

entries.name: Cargo Mag-Sled
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: chest minecart carries one chest
entries.summary: Freight sled auto-loading and unloading at tube ports and chests, moving three stacks per trip.

entries.name: Boarding Gate
entries.summary: Platform gate seating a waiting player into the next arriving cart automatically.

entries.name: Signal Semaphore
entries.summary: Block-section signals holding following carts until the section ahead clears, preventing rear-end collisions.

entries.name: Maglev Elevator
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla vertical rail; bubble columns lift slowly
entries.summary: Vertical mag-rail shaft lifting carts and riders straight up at charge cost.

entries.name: Route Programmer Desk
entries.summary: Drafting desk with a map-style UI producing Route Card items (own codec, up to 16 waypoints) that carts carry and junctions obey — a contract fully separate from golem punch cards.

entries.name: Crossing Chime
entries.summary: Level-crossing warning flashing and ringing when a cart approaches.

entries.name: Grand Central Beacon
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: beacon beam is the vanilla landmark
entries.summary: Station landmark projecting a named-station HUD marker to approaching riders through fog and night.

entries.name: Freight Weighbridge
entries.summary: Track scale weighing passing sleds, emitting comparator output and appending entries to a paper manifest log.

entries.name: Loop Rail
entries.summary: Inverted and vertical-loop rail segments with magnetic grip for carts only — roller-coaster spectacle.

entries.name: Brass Cortex
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: the vanilla copper golem only sorts copper-chest items
entries.summary: Craftable cortex item whose use-ritual converts a vanilla copper golem in place into a programmable Cuprum Automaton (the survival acquisition path for the U12 system).

entries.name: Programming Bench
entries.summary: Workstation with a visual node-graph editor for building automaton programs and writing them onto punch cards.

entries.name: Golem Punch Cards
entries.summary: Portable golem program cards (up to 32 ops), insertable, copyable and tradable (a golem-program contract; rail Route Cards are a separate RAIL-10 codec).

entries.name: Harvester Routine
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: villagers farm only their own crops
entries.summary: Routine op-set: detect mature crops in a marked area, harvest, replant and deposit produce.

entries.name: Lumberjack Routine
entries.summary: Routine op-set: fell marked trees top-down and replant matching saplings, delivering logs to storage.

entries.name: Miner Routine
entries.summary: Routine op-set: mine only detector-marked vein blocks inside a bounded region, returning ores.

entries.name: Courier Routine
entries.summary: Routine op-set: carry item batches between named containers or tube ports on a waypoint route.

entries.name: Guard Routine
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: iron golem fights on its own AI
entries.summary: Routine op-set: patrol waypoints, alert on spotting hostiles and retreat toward cover.

entries.name: Fisher Routine
entries.summary: Routine op-set: cast and reel at a marked water waypoint, banking catches automatically.

entries.name: Waypoint Flag
entries.summary: Placeable named markers forming the path graph automata navigate between.

entries.name: Charging Perch
entries.summary: Dock where automata recharge and idle with a sleep animation, resuming programs when refilled.

entries.name: Golem Toolkit
entries.summary: Inspection tool: repair, rename and read a live program trace showing where a program failed.

entries.name: Backpack Frame
entries.summary: Bolt-on frame expanding an automaton's inventory from 9 to 27 slots.

entries.name: Oxide Clock
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: daylight sensor gives raw signals
entries.summary: Scheduler block broadcasting timed start/stop cues to selected automata — shift work for the fleet.

entries.name: Relay Whistle
entries.summary: Owner's whistle summoning a bound automaton and issuing quick orders from a radial menu at range.

entries.name: Trade Stall
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: villager trading exists; player-stocked shops do not
entries.summary: Shop stall staffed by your automaton selling owner-stocked items at set prices while you are away, with escrow-safe transactions rate-limited to 4 purchases/s per player.

entries.name: Error Semaphore
entries.summary: Faulted automata raise a visible flag and beep a pattern identifying the fault class from across the base.

entries.name: Parade Mode
entries.summary: Cosmetic formation command marching multiple golems in synchronized parade step.

entries.name: Blueprint Card Library
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: the chiseled bookshelf stores books and the 1.21.9 shelf displays items; the library adds labeled program storage plus copying
entries.summary: Library bookcase storing, labeling and copying punch cards, making program collections shareable.

entries.name: Ancient Automaton Frame
entries.summary: Rare chassis found in vanilla ancient city chests and trial chamber vaults, restorable into an elite automaton with a second card slot and larger buffer.

entries.name: Barometer Block
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: daylight sensor reads light, nothing forecasts
entries.summary: Dial block predicting weather changes ahead of time and emitting a comparator code for automation.

entries.name: Storm Siren
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: bell warns of raids only
entries.summary: Siren that howls once when a thunderstorm begins within its alert radius.

entries.name: Cloud Seeder Rocket
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: only /weather changes weather outside U21
entries.summary: Consumable rocket fired skyward to start rain. Explicitly **dimension-global** (the sanctioned exception to the bounded-radius rule): requires OP or `cuprum.weather.use`, is server-authoritative, and enforces a 36,000-tick per-dimension cooldown.

entries.name: Hygrometer Charm
entries.summary: Pocket charm showing current humidity and the resulting oxidation-speed multiplier on the HUD.

entries.name: Storm Glass
entries.summary: Decorative crystal bottle whose contents visibly swirl and grow crystals as storms approach — pure forecasting ambiance (information duplicated from WEA-01, hence stretch).

entries.name: Fulgurite
entries.summary: Lightning strikes on sand or soil leave a fulgurite block that smelts into glass plus charge shards — harvestable storm residue consumed by TOOL-08 and QOL-11 recipes.

entries.name: Wind Sock
entries.summary: Pole-mounted sock rendering the live global wind direction and strength that turbines, kites and fans obey.

entries.name: Gust Events
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: breeze wind charges push locally
entries.summary: Telegraphed random gusts sweep loaded areas, pushing exposed entities and briefly boosting turbines.

entries.name: Rainwater Cistern
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: cauldron fills slowly with rain
entries.summary: Roof cistern collecting rain as usable water while a droplet wheel trickle-generates charge and supplies humidity to the Weathering Chamber.

entries.name: Snow Cannon
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: snow golem trails snow passively
entries.summary: Charged cannon spraying snow layers in a cone — instant winter terrain and a slow-field against mobs.

entries.name: Heatwave Emitter
entries.summary: Area dryer that melts snow, evaporates puddles and boosts Thermo Junctions, with a real grass-fire risk.

entries.name: Storm Chaser's Compass
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: recovery/lodestone compasses point to fixed spots
entries.summary: Compass swinging toward the next natural lightning strike location during storms — the storm-chaser tool.

entries.name: Aurora Projector
entries.summary: Charge-fed projector painting aurora ribbons across the night sky above the base — sky spectacle.

entries.name: Hail Volley
entries.summary: Manipulator add-on unleashing a brief local hail barrage that hammers exposed hostiles — shelter suddenly matters (radius-bounded, permission-gated like all weather actions).

entries.name: Twin-Hook Rig
entries.summary: Grapple upgrade adding a second hook for alternating two-point swings and mid-air redirects.

entries.name: Zipline Anchor Kit
entries.summary: Anchor pairs strung with wire that players ride with a handle — fixed-route canyon crossings.

entries.name: Magnet Glove
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: nothing pulls drops; hoppers collect passively
entries.summary: Glove vacuuming nearby item drops to you and yanking metal levers and iron doors from range (never container contents; the target position must be edit-permitted; actuations rate-limited 4/s).

entries.name: Prospector's Seismograph
entries.summary: Deployable sensor thumping the ground and printing a Survey Map of ore density for the surrounding region, using bounded main-thread scanning.

entries.name: Tuning Forks
entries.summary: Fork set attuning the Ore Detector to one ore type, with ping pitch encoding distance bands.

entries.name: Copper Compass
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: vanilla compasses point to spawn/lodestone
entries.summary: Maintenance compass pointing to the nearest Cuprum machine in a fault state.

entries.name: Pocket Fan
entries.summary: Handheld fan gusting mobs and projectiles away and slowing your fall when aimed downward.

entries.name: Vent Boots
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: wind charge jumps are the vanilla analogue
entries.summary: Boots with a compressed-air double jump, recharged at plinths or from carried batteries; crafted with 2 charge shards.

entries.name: Copper Snips
entries.summary: Precision snips harvesting tubes and wires instantly without scatter and cleanly detaching leads and armor-stand gear.

entries.name: Blueprint Scanner
entries.summary: Scanner capturing a built region (up to 32³) into a Ghost Blueprint item for survival-legal rebuilding; scanning requires edit permission over the region.

entries.name: Ghost Projector
entries.summary: Projector rendering a hologram of a blueprint in-world with per-block placement hints until the build completes.

entries.name: XP Condenser
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: bottles o' enchanting are creative/trade only
entries.summary: Machine condensing XP-drone deposits into bottled Amber Charge for storage and trade.

entries.name: Echo Whistle
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: spectral arrow marks a single hit target
entries.summary: Sonar whistle briefly outlining all entities through nearby walls — audio-first recon that also serves low-vision players.

entries.name: Plate Calibration Kit
entries.summary: Field kit reprogramming a weighted plate's trigger: player-only, hostile-only, or a weight threshold using the mod's entity-weight table (player 60, zombie 60, item 1).

entries.name: Foam Sprayer
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: water bucket is the only fire response
entries.summary: Charge-fed extinguisher spraying decaying foam that kills fire, coats magma and knocks back blazes.

entries.name: Copper Chisel
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: axe scrapes one full oxidation stage
entries.summary: Chisel carving eight patina motifs into copper blocks and shaving oxidation off layer by layer.

entries.name: Insulated Gloves
entries.summary: Work gloves negating shock from live wires and fences and enabling safe hot-wiring of powered networks.

entries.name: Coil Driver
entries.summary: Endgame rail-technology precision rifle whose slug overpenetrates lines of mobs at heavy charge cost.

entries.name: Pocket Golem
entries.summary: Figurine deploying a temporary mini-automaton that fetches one marked item stack and returns before expiring.

entries.name: Storm-in-a-Bottle
entries.summary: Bottled storm burst captured during thunderstorms; smash it anywhere for one local lightning strike on demand.

entries.name: Copper Kazoo
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: goat horn plays one sample
entries.summary: Joke instrument playing a procedurally generated jingle that makes nearby passive mobs bob in a brief dance.

entries.name: Circuit Probe Goggles
entries.summary: Head-slot goggles rendering floating redstone signal strengths and charge values over wires and machines in view.

entries.name: Frame Chassis I–III
entries.summary: Chassis progression defining socket counts and the total power budget for all mounted modules.

entries.name: Sprint Servos
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: speed potions/soul speed are the analogues
entries.summary: Leg module adding sprint speed and automatic one-block step-assist while charged.

entries.name: Shock Absorbers
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: feather falling reduces, never negates
entries.summary: Leg module negating fall damage and converting hard landings into a small AoE stagger slam.

entries.name: Power Fists
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: haste/knockback effects are the analogues
entries.summary: Arm module boosting mining speed and adding heavy knockback to punches.

entries.name: Precision Actuators
entries.summary: Arm module speeding item use, crossbow reload and bow draw — the utility-handling loadout.

entries.name: Capacitor Hump
entries.summary: Back module holding a large personal charge buffer that feeds all other modules and connected gadgets (mutually exclusive with U17 and EXO-07 in the back socket).

entries.name: Glider Vanes
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: elytra is full flight; vanes are glide-only
entries.summary: Back module unfolding copper-feather vanes for controlled gliding; U13 fan updrafts extend range, rockets do nothing.

entries.name: Rib Dynamo
entries.summary: Chest module toggle converting hunger into emergency charge when batteries run dry.

entries.name: Ore Optics
entries.summary: Helm module embedding the ore detector as a periodic wearable pulse marking nearby veins hands-free.

entries.name: Verdigris Ablative Plating
entries.summary: Armor plating whose patina layer absorbs a hit-point pool and regrows in rain — living armor charged via the OXI-08 bath.

entries.name: Coolant Loop
entries.summary: Support module dissipating module heat so sustained use throttles later — the enabler of heavy loadouts.

entries.name: Maintenance Rack
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: armor stand displays, never services
entries.summary: Dock station repairing exo parts with charge and offering drag-and-drop loadout reconfiguration.

entries.name: Overdrive Core
entries.summary: Socketed burst core: ten seconds of boosted modules followed by a hard exhaustion debuff and heat spike.

entries.name: Mag-Boots
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: spider climbing is mob-only; no player wall-walk
entries.summary: Boot module gripping copper and iron surfaces for brief wall and ceiling walking, chaining with U19 wall-wire routes.

entries.name: Spark Wisp
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: allay is the nearest ambient helper; wisps are catchable energy motes
entries.summary: Drifting storm-spawned mote catchable in a glass jar to serve as a portable light and trickle charge source.

entries.name: Oxide Creeper
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: creeper variant; charged creeper is the vanilla precedent
entries.summary: Creeper variant whose blast oxidizes nearby copper a full stage and corrodes armor (canonical U06 Corroded) instead of cratering terrain; its patina dust drop feeds an optional alternative extract recipe registered with this feature (the OXI-04 vanilla bootstrap stays independent).

entries.name: Rust Mite
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: silverfish behavior template; the machine-eating niche is new
entries.summary: Pest that gnaws unwaxed Cuprum machines toward fault states until squashed; waxed machines are immune — upkeep pressure with a cheap answer.

entries.name: Storm Elemental
entries.summary: High-altitude miniboss materializing in severe thunderstorms; drops the Storm Heart that powers the U21 overdrive mode (ADV-04).

entries.name: Junk Golem
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: iron golem is friendly; junk golem is a hostile scrap construct
entries.summary: Shambling scrap construct haunting ruins that hurls debris and drops machine components; its cuprite scrap recycles at the OXI-15 casting table via a recipe registered with this feature.

entries.name: Copper Peacock
entries.summary: Passive bird whose tail oxidizes through patina stages, shedding Patina Feathers; feathers feed an optional alternative extract recipe (4 feathers → 1 OXI-04 extract) alongside OXI-04's own vanilla-material bootstrap.

entries.name: Magnetized Zombie
entries.summary: Zombie variant whose hits can yank the metal weapon from your hands unless you wear insulated grips.

entries.name: Electrician Villager
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: new villager profession like librarian
entries.summary: Villager profession keyed to the Alloy Kiln jobsite, trading components, blank punch cards and survey maps across five levels.

entries.name: Rogue Unit
entries.summary: Corrupted automaton lurking in dungeons that fights with tool arms but can be hacked with the Golem Toolkit into a loyal unit.

entries.name: Leyden Slime
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: slime variant with charge mechanics
entries.summary: Slime soaking up ambient charge, splitting with arc bursts and dropping conductive gel (gel is an optional double-duration substitute for paste in FX-14 brews — optional integration, never required).

entries.name: Thundercloud Ray
entries.summary: Majestic manta gliding inside storm cells that a daring player can grapple onto for a brief, wild sky ride.

entries.name: Museum Curator
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: wandering trader is the nearest roaming NPC
entries.summary: Structure-bound curator NPC issuing artifact bounties and rewarding donated collections with cosmetic auras.

entries.name: Weathered Foundry
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla industrial ruin exists
entries.summary: Overworld ruin structure containing a broken kiln, loot and lore books teaching the mod's early progression, guarded by junk golems.

entries.name: Lightning Fields
entries.vanilla_overlap: none: no vanilla region has strike bias
entries.summary: Basalt-and-spire badlands region where natural strikes cluster — the natural home for early storm harvesting, littered with harvestable fulgurite.

entries.name: Copper Viaduct Ruins
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: ruined portals are the vanilla teaser analogue
entries.summary: Ruined mag-rail viaduct fragments crossing valleys, restorable into working line segments — worldgen that teaches RAIL. Every fragment set fits within 96 blocks of its structure center (inside the 128-block jigsaw cap); separate placements may visually align across a valley but each structure is independent.

entries.name: Stormwatch Spire
entries.summary: Mountaintop watchtower with a barometer altar and a treasure map leading toward the Storm Elemental's arena region.

entries.name: Malachite Geodes
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: amethyst geodes are the vanilla template
entries.summary: Deepslate geodes of malachite crystal; shards cut into decorative gem blocks and the polarizing lenses used by DEC-13 holo-signs.

entries.name: Verdigris Buds
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: amethyst buds grow on budding stone
entries.summary: Malachite buds that grow only on copper blocks fed with trickle charge — renewable crystal farming for powered bases.

entries.name: Sunken Cable Ship
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: shipwrecks exist; this variant carries industrial cargo
entries.summary: Ocean shipwreck variant whose holds carry wire coils, fluid capsules and tube segments — marine salvage for the logistics age.

entries.name: Nomad Electrician Camp
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: wandering trader camps do not exist in vanilla
entries.summary: Small tented camp housing an electrician villager with starter trades and a lit lamp post built from the vanilla 1.21.9 copper bulb (available at W11; no DEC dependency) — a roaming vendor beachhead.

entries.name: Buried Charge Cache
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: buried treasure with maps is the vanilla template
entries.summary: Underground vault of pre-charged PWR-06 small cells behind a shock-trapped door; TOOL-04 survey maps mark cache tiles.

entries.name: Oxidized Canyon
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: badlands strata are the vanilla template
entries.summary: Layered canyon region streaked with patina bands and dense exposed copper — the copper rush destination.

entries.name: Aurora Grove
entries.summary: Rare high-latitude grove where natural auroras appear at night; WEA-13 projectors placed inside run at half upkeep (optional synergy, no recipe gating either way).

entries.name: Machinist's Vault
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: trial chambers gate loot behind combat; this gates behind puzzle wiring
entries.summary: Sealed puzzle vault whose door opens only when its three charge sockets are wired to simultaneous supply — a wiring exam with treasure.

entries.name: Corrosion Marsh
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: swamp exists; the corrosion hazard layer is new
entries.summary: Acid-humid marsh where gear oxidizes at double speed and rust mites swarm — high-pressure region with matching rewards.

entries.name: Sky Bolide Event
entries.summary: Rare telegraphed night event: a copper bolide streaks down and stamps one small crater of ores and noble copper — bounded, transactional, loot-worthy, and incapable of touching player builds.

entries.name: Overload Shock
entries.vanilla_overlap: none: extends the mod's own U03 registration
entries.summary: Separate stackable W12 effect layered over the canonical U03 Shock: heavy arc sources (overcharged turret shots, lightning, ≥300 Cg discharges) additionally apply a longer stun that disables shield blocking — the base U03 Shock still applies unchanged and is never replaced or re-registered.

entries.name: Deep Corrosion
entries.vanilla_overlap: none: extends the mod's own U06 registration
entries.summary: W12 extension of the canonical U06 Corroded: at Corroded II, incoming hits additionally bleed armor durability — sustained corrosion pressure without changing U06's level cap or duration.

entries.name: Grounded
entries.summary: Buff from copper-sole gear and certain foods: immune to the canonical U03 Shock and knockback-resistant while it lasts.

entries.name: Static Charge
entries.summary: Personal charge meter that builds as you sprint over wool/carpet and discharges a small zap into the next entity you touch.

entries.name: Conductive
entries.summary: Debuff on wet or gel-coated targets making them bridge electric damage to other Conductive targets nearby.

entries.name: Voltaic Edge
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla enchant consumes energy
entries.summary: Weapon enchantment (I–III) adding charge-fed arc damage per swing and marking victims for turret priority focus.

entries.name: Insulating Tread
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: frost walker/soul speed are the boot analogues
entries.summary: Boot enchantment negating shock floors, TES-04 fence contact and static buildup — the mobility answer to electric denial.

entries.name: Galvanized
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: haste exists; this is charge-fueled
entries.summary: Buff from charged wearables: mining speed rises while your batteries hold charge — industry runs on power, literally.

entries.name: Magnetized Pickup
entries.summary: Tool enchantment teleporting mined drops and XP orbs straight into your inventory.

entries.name: Patina Ward
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: mending repairs via XP; this repairs via rain
entries.summary: Armor enchantment: patina layers self-repair gear during rain — weather-linked durability upkeep.

entries.name: Capacitor
entries.summary: Armor enchantment adding personal charge buffer per piece, feeding modules and gadgets before your battery pack.

entries.name: Corroding Curse
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: vanilla curses exist; this one is cleansable by gameplay
entries.summary: Weapon-borne curse from rogue units that slowly rusts the victim's held gear until cleansed at a Waxing Station cycle.

entries.name: Verdigris Cake
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: cake is the placeable-food template
entries.summary: Celebration cake whose seven slices each grant a party buff bundle (Grounded plus minor speed) — a base-warming ritual.

entries.name: Bottled Conductivity
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: potion system carries the new effects
entries.summary: Brewable potion line applying Conductive on hit (splash/lingering variants) — the setup tool for chain-lightning plays; conductive gel (MOB-10, stretch) is an optional substitute giving double duration when present, never required.

entries.name: Stormcaller's Rod
entries.summary: Ceremonial weapon slamming down a real lightning strike at the struck point during thunderstorms, with a long cooldown.

entries.name: Ozone Bloom
entries.summary: Rare post-boss weather aftermath: a crisp ozone-charged zone where generation runs hot for a few minutes.

entries.name: Coppersmith Tree
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: vanilla advancement UI hosts a custom tab
entries.summary: Full advancement tab walking players from first copper to storm mastery with staged reward pouches.

entries.name: Milestone Unlocks
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: recipe unlocks exist for vanilla recipes
entries.summary: Key machines reveal their recipes upon reaching story milestones, keeping the recipe book readable early.

entries.name: Handbook Quest Chain
entries.summary: Guided handbook quest line whose steps auto-complete by real play actions, teaching charge, shields and automation hands-on.

entries.name: Storm Heart Overdrive
entries.summary: The Storm Heart (MOB-04 drop) crafts the Overdrive Core, an insert that unlocks the weather manipulator's overdrive mode: half-cost storm summons plus the severe-storm option. Base U21 remains fully craftable and usable without it.

entries.name: Relic Collection
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla collection meta exists
entries.summary: Museum pedestal set for six unique structure artifacts; completing the display grants a permanent cosmetic aura.

entries.name: Statistics Codex
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: vanilla stats screen counts generic actions
entries.summary: Handbook chapter tracking mod-specific lifetime stats (charge generated, strikes captured, golems built) with per-stat milestones.

entries.name: Mastery Discounts
entries.summary: Repeated machine use grants per-machine mastery ranks that shave charge costs — visible expertise.

entries.name: Prospector's Map Compass
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: recovery compass points to death; this points to discovery
entries.summary: First-join starter compass pointing to the nearest Weathered Foundry, funneling new players into the teaching structure.

entries.name: Elder Electrician Trades
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: villager master trades are the template
entries.summary: After the overdrive milestone, master electricians add an elder trade tier selling advanced blueprints for core machines (PWR-14, SHD-10, TES-08 discount recipes).

entries.name: Legacy Vault
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla death-XP insurance exists
entries.summary: Chunk-loaded vault block banking a portion of your XP on death for later withdrawal — insurance with infrastructure cost.

entries.name: Patina Panel Set
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: cut copper exists; panels add connected-motif surfaces
entries.summary: Chiseled wall panel family whose motifs visually connect across neighbors into large murals — a building set with seam logic.

entries.name: Filigree Glass
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: stained glass is inert; this conducts
entries.summary: Copper-laced glass panes that pass charge like a thin wire — visible circuitry as architecture.

entries.name: Verdigris Roof Tiles
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: stairs/slabs exist; tiles weather and sound
entries.summary: Roof tile stairs/slabs that weather through patina stages and ping melodically under rain — living roofs with the full oxidation life cycle.

entries.name: Copper Gutter
entries.summary: Roofline channel catching rain and routing it into cisterns — functional drainage that doubles the fill rate.

entries.name: Arc Lamp Series
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: copper_bulb has 4 oxidation-fixed brightnesses toggled by redstone; arc lamps scale with live supply
entries.summary: Streetlight/sconce/chandelier family whose brightness scales with supplied charge and that browns out with the grid — city lighting on the network.

entries.name: Copper Radiator
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: the vanilla 1.21 copper grate is a decorative waterloggable block with no heat function
entries.summary: Charge-fed warming fixture: melts nearby snow and ice, dries the floor and gives golems a cozy idle spot — ambient heating as a building element (distinct from TUB-12's tube-network pressure valve).

entries.name: Chime Carillon
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: note block plays single notes; carillon plays scores
entries.summary: Wind-and-power bell tower instrument playing note scores from golem punch cards — automated melody as architecture.

entries.name: Bulletin Board
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: signs hold 4 fixed lines; boards hold formatted notices
entries.summary: Large multi-tile notice board holding formatted text pages (optionally displays a PWR-23 teleprinter feed when that stretch feature is present — never required).

entries.name: Weathervane
entries.summary: Rooftop vane pointing the live global wind direction with a comparator output for wind-aware automation.

entries.name: Gear Wall Kinetics
entries.summary: Decorative gear-and-shaft wall sets that visibly spin only while real charge flows behind them — status display as ornament.

entries.name: Patina Fresco Canvas
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: paintings are fixed art; frescos are player-painted
entries.summary: Wall canvas painted with patina extract in sixteen verdigris hues — pixel art that is also a copper sink.

entries.name: Workshop Furniture
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla chairs/tables; barrels/chests are the only storage furniture
entries.summary: Copper-framed chairs, workbenches and part drawers — sittable seating and small sorted storage with mechanical flavor.

entries.name: Holo-Sign Projector
entries.summary: Projector rendering floating holographic text or an item icon above it through a malachite lens — futuristic signage.

entries.name: Fountain Core
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: water physics exists; sculpted jets do not
entries.summary: Charged fountain nozzle sculpting water into programmable jet patterns with splash sounds — plaza centerpieces.

entries.name: Storm Chimes
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: no vanilla wind-reactive sound block
entries.summary: Hanging copper chimes that tinkle with the wind vector and ring a bright chord when storm fronts arrive — ambient forecasting.

entries.name: Museum Display Case
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: item frames show items; cases add provenance and auras
entries.summary: Sealed display case with engraved plaque and soft aura beams for exhibiting trophies like the Storm Heart.

entries.name: Handbook Quick-Access
entries.summary: Keybind plus a "?" button on every mod GUI deep-linking into the exact handbook chapter for the machine at hand.

entries.name: Machine GUI Standard
entries.summary: Unified GUI layout for all machines: charge bar, IO side configurator, upgrade slots and status lamp always in the same places.

entries.name: Wrench
entries.summary: The mod's one adjustment tool: rotate machines, toggle IO faces, and sneak-click to pick up machines with inventory and settings intact — owner/team-permitted only, executed as one atomic transaction.

entries.name: Reduced Effects Mode
entries.summary: One config toggle swapping every T1 shader effect to its T2/T3 fallback and capping particle counts — playability on weak hardware and for photosensitive players.

entries.name: Colorblind Indicator Set
entries.summary: Alternative indicator skin replacing color-only states with shapes and patterns (charge = diagonal fill, fault = cross-hatch) across HUD, GUIs and block lamps.

entries.name: Audio Mixer Categories
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: vanilla sound sliders are coarse categories
entries.summary: Dedicated volume sliders for mod sound groups (machines, arcs, ambience, alarms) with alarm-priority ducking.

entries.name: REI Integration
entries.summary: Full recipe-viewer support: kiln alloys, refinery yields, casting molds and charge costs browsable in REI.

entries.name: Charge HUD Widget
entries.summary: Optional corner HUD showing carried battery charge, nearest-network status and active module drain — the at-a-glance power dashboard.

entries.name: Network Overview Map
entries.summary: Handbook-embedded live diagram of your charge networks: nodes, flows, faults — SimCity for your copper grid, scoped to networks you own or share via team.

entries.name: Diagnostics Overlay
entries.vanilla_overlap: extends: F3 exists; this is mod-scoped
entries.summary: OP-gated read-only debug feed listing per-system tick costs, entity counts and network stats — the server owner's tuning lens.

entries.name: Copper Flare
entries.vanilla_overlap: adjacent: firework stars are uncontrolled; this is a team ping
entries.summary: Team signal flare shooting a colored, named beam column visible to teammates at long range — cheap coordination (crafted from 2 charge shards; launches rate-limited to 1 per player per 100 ticks).

entries.name: Statistics Dashboard
entries.summary: Wall-mounted base dashboard cycling live graphs of charge generation, storage and consumption over the last game day — monitoring as furniture (an optional GEN-05 lens variant adds a magnified gauge face).
